news:
Premium wood lockers at unbeatable prices. Price match guarantee.
FOLLOW US: f v t P in
  • HOME
  • SHOP
  • SPORT
  • BY SPORT
    • Football
    • Hockey
    • Basketball
    • Baseball
    • Soccer
    • Lacrosse
  • GALLERY

PLAYERSTALL
  • OUR PROCESS
  • BLOG
  • CONTACT
  • CART 0
February 8, 2026 · 5 min read Uncategorized

<!--

&lt;ul&gt; &lt;li&gt;Helmet shelves with ventilation&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;Wide compartments for shoulder pads&lt;/li&gt; &lt;li&gt;Ventilated shoe compartments&

<ul>

<li>Helmet shelves with ventilation</li>

<li>Wide compartments for shoulder pads</li>

<li>Ventilated shoe compartments</li>

<li>Hanging space for multiple uniforms</li>

<li>Secure storage for personal items</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Basketball Equipment Storage:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Ventilated shoe storage</li>

<li>Uniform hanging space</li>

<li>Small compartments for personal items</li>

<li>Minimal equipment needs (simpler configuration)</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Baseball Equipment Storage:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Separate bat storage (often wall-mounted racks)</li>

<li>Glove storage compartments</li>

<li>Multiple cleat storage (game, practice, turf)</li>

<li>Uniform organization (home, away, practice)</li>

<li>Catcher's equipment storage (larger compartments)</li>

</ul>

<p>Custom wood lockers can be configured for any sport's specific needs, ensuring optimal organization and equipment protection.</p>

<h2 id="budget-planning">Budget Planning for College Programs</h2>

<h3>The Real Cost of a Locker Room Project</h3>

<p>Here's where programs get into trouble: they look at the per-locker price and think that's the total cost. It's not. Not even close.</p>

<p>Let me break down what a real project actually costs. Say you're doing 100 lockers at our Varsity tier ($449 per locker). That's $44,900 for the lockers themselves. But that's just the beginning.</p>

<p>You've also got installation—usually $50-$150 per locker depending on how complex it is. Are you installing on concrete? Do you need to run electrical? Is the space already prepped? For most projects, figure around $100 per locker for installation, so that's another $10,000.</p>

<p>Then there's shipping. We're in Canada, so shipping to the U.S. runs $20-$50 per locker depending on where you are and how many you're ordering. Let's say $30 per locker average, so that's $3,000.</p>

<p>Site preparation is the wild card. Sometimes your space is ready to go. Sometimes you need new flooring, electrical work, HVAC upgrades, or other prep work. We've seen site prep costs range from $5,000 to $20,000. Let's use $10,000 as a middle ground.</p>

<p>And always, always budget 10-15% for contingency. Something always comes up. Maybe the space measurements were off. Maybe you need extra electrical work. Maybe there's a delay and you need temporary storage. Budget 10% contingency, which is another $6,790.</p>

<p>So your total project cost for 100 Varsity tier lockers? $74,690. Not $44,900.</p>

<p>I can't tell you how many times we've had programs come to us with a $50,000 budget thinking they can do 100 lockers, only to realize they need $75,000. Plan for the full cost upfront, and you'll avoid those awkward conversations with administration when you need more money halfway through the project.</p>

<h3>Budget Allocation Strategies</h3>

<p>College programs use various strategies to allocate locker room budgets across sports. Here are the most effective approaches:</p>

<p><strong>Strategy 1: Revenue Sport Priority</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Invest premium lockers in revenue sports (football, basketball)</li>

<li>Use cost-effective options for non-revenue sports</li>

<li>Maximizes recruiting impact where it matters most</li>

<li>Best for programs with limited budgets</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Strategy 2: Equal Investment</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Same tier lockers for all sports</li>

<li>Promotes equity across programs</li>

<li>Simplifies decision-making</li>

<li>Best for programs with adequate budgets</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Strategy 3: Phased Approach</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Complete revenue sports first (Year 1)</li>

<li>Add non-revenue sports in subsequent years</li>

<li>Spreads cost over multiple budget cycles</li>

<li>Best for programs with multi-year planning</li>

</ul>

<p>Our free custom design consultation helps you evaluate these strategies and choose the approach that best fits your program's priorities and budget constraints.</p>

<h3>Funding Sources</h3>

<p>College programs fund locker room projects through various sources. Understanding these options helps you plan and secure necessary resources.</p>

<p><strong>Common Funding Sources:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Athletic Department Budget:</strong> Annual operating budget allocation</li>

<li><strong>Capital Improvement Funds:</strong> Multi-year facility improvement budgets</li>

<li><strong>Donor Contributions:</strong> Alumni or booster donations specifically for facilities</li>

<li><strong>Bond Issues:</strong> Long-term financing for major facility projects</li>

<li><strong>Conference Revenue Sharing:</strong> Distribution from conference media deals</li>

<li><strong>Naming Rights:</strong> Corporate sponsorships for facility naming</li>

</ul>

<p>Many programs combine multiple funding sources to complete locker room projects. Our team can help you prepare cost estimates and project justifications for funding requests.</p>

<h2 id="tier-selection">Choosing the Right Product Tier</h2>

<h3>Our Five Tiers: Which One Fits Your Program?</h3>

<p>We've got five different tiers, and honestly, most programs use a mix of them. Here's the breakdown:</p>

<p><strong>Semi Pro ($349/locker):</strong> This is our entry-level tier, but don't let "entry-level" fool you. It's still full 3/4" birch construction—solid wood, not particle board. You get standard finishes and hardware, basic customization, and the same 15-20+ year lifespan as our higher tiers. We see a lot of non-revenue sports go with this tier, or programs that are really budget-conscious. It's a great option if you need quality but can't justify the extra cost for premium finishes.</p>

<p><strong>Varsity ($449/locker):</strong> This is probably our most popular tier. You get enhanced finishes, better hardware, team color matching, and a more professional appearance. It's the sweet spot for a lot of programs—professional enough for recruiting, but not so expensive that it breaks the budget. We see a lot of mid-tier Division I and Division II programs use this for their main sports.</p>

<p><strong>Pro ($549/locker):</strong> Now we're talking premium. Full customization capabilities, integrated team branding, player name engraving—the works. This is what we recommend for revenue sports at programs that are serious about recruiting. If your locker room is your "number one recruiting tool," this is probably the tier you want.</p>

<p><strong>Curve ($649/locker):</strong> This one's unique—literally. It's got a curved front design that looks different from anything else out there. Same premium materials and customization as Pro tier, just with a distinctive aesthetic. We've had a few programs go with this when they want something that really stands out. It's not for everyone, but if you want unique, this is it.</p>

<p><strong>Stadium ($599/locker):</strong> Professional-grade everything. Maximum durability, full customization suite, premium appearance. This is what top-tier programs and professional facilities use. It's built to last and built to impress.</p>

<p>Most programs don't pick one tier and use it everywhere. They mix and match—Pro tier for football, Varsity for basketball, Semi Pro for everything else. That's totally fine, and honestly, it's usually the smartest approach. Our free consultation helps you figure out which tier makes sense for each sport.</p>

<h3>Tier Selection Framework</h3>

<p>Use this framework to select the appropriate tier for each sport:</p>

<p><strong>Considerations:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Recruiting Impact:</strong> How important is this facility for recruiting?</li>

<li><strong>Budget Constraints:</strong> What's the available budget?</li>

<li><strong>Usage Level:</strong> How frequently is the facility used?</li>

<li><strong>Program Priorities:</strong> What are the program's strategic priorities?</li>

<li><strong>Long-Term Plans:</strong> Are there future expansion plans?</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Decision Matrix:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li><strong>High Recruiting Impact + Adequate Budget:</strong> Pro or Stadium tier</li>

<li><strong>Moderate Recruiting Impact + Limited Budget:</strong> Varsity tier</li>

<li><strong>Low Recruiting Impact + Tight Budget:</strong> Semi Pro tier</li>

<li><strong>Unique Design Requirements:</strong> Curve tier</li>

</ul>

<p>Many programs use different tiers for different sports, maximizing value while meeting varied needs across their athletic programs.</p>

<h2 id="sport-specific">Sport-Specific Locker Requirements</h2>

<h3>Football Locker Requirements</h3>

<p>Football programs have the most complex equipment storage needs. Custom wood lockers for football must accommodate:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Helmets:</strong> Ventilated shelves, secure storage, easy access</li>

<li><strong>Shoulder Pads:</strong> Wide compartments (24" minimum width recommended)</li>

<li><strong>Cleats:</strong> Multiple pairs (game, practice, turf), ventilated storage</li>

<li><strong>Uniforms:</strong> Multiple sets (home, away, practice), hanging space</li>

<li><strong>Protective Gear:</strong> Knee pads, elbow pads, gloves, organized storage</li>

<li><strong>Personal Items:</strong> Secure storage for phones, wallets, keys</li>

</ul>

<p>Our football lockers typically feature 24" width, adjustable shelving, ventilated compartments, and ample hanging space to accommodate all equipment types.</p>

<h3>Basketball Locker Requirements</h3>

<p>Basketball programs have simpler equipment needs but still require professional storage solutions:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Basketball Shoes:</strong> Ventilated storage, multiple pairs</li>

<li><strong>Uniforms:</strong> Home and away sets, hanging space</li>

<li><strong>Practice Gear:</strong> Shorts, jerseys, warm-ups</li>

<li><strong>Personal Items:</strong> Secure storage</li>

</ul>

<p>Basketball lockers can be narrower (18" width) since equipment is less bulky, allowing more lockers in the same space or cost savings on materials.</p>

<h3>Baseball Locker Requirements</h3>

<p>Baseball programs need specialized storage for unique equipment:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Bats:</strong> Separate bat racks (wall-mounted or integrated)</li>

<li><strong>Gloves:</strong> Proper storage to maintain shape</li>

<li><strong>Cleats:</strong> Multiple pairs (game, practice, turf)</li>

<li><strong>Uniforms:</strong> Home, away, practice sets</li>

<li><strong>Catcher's Equipment:</strong> Larger compartments for bulky gear</li>

</ul>

<p>Baseball lockers often include integrated bat storage or work with separate bat rack systems to accommodate equipment needs.</p>

<h3>Hockey Locker Requirements</h3>

<p>Hockey programs have unique equipment storage challenges:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Skates:</strong> Ventilated storage, proper support</li>

<li><strong>Sticks:</strong> Separate stick racks (wall-mounted)</li>

<li><strong>Pads:</strong> Large compartments for bulky protective gear</li>

<li><strong>Uniforms:</strong> Home and away sets</li>

<li><strong>Ventilation:</strong> Critical for wet equipment drying</li>

</ul>

<p>Hockey lockers require enhanced ventilation systems and often work with separate stick rack installations to accommodate all equipment.</p>

<h2 id="recruiting-impact">Recruiting Impact & Facility Value</h2>

<h3>The Recruiting Reality</h3>

<p>College recruiting is more competitive than ever. Top athletes receive multiple scholarship offers and visit several programs before making decisions. Facilities—especially locker rooms—play a crucial role in these decisions.</p>

<p>After 30+ years working with college programs, we've heard countless coaches describe their locker room as their "number one recruiting tool." When recruits walk into a facility with custom wood lockers featuring team branding, player names, and professional finishes, they see a program that invests in athlete success.</p>

<p><strong>Recruiting Impact Data:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Programs with professional locker facilities report 30-40% higher official visit conversion rates</li>

<li>Top recruits consistently rank facilities among top 3 decision factors</li>

<li>Social media sharing of locker room tours generates significant recruiting value</li>

<li>Facility improvements correlate with improved recruiting class rankings</li>

</ul>

<p>Custom wood lockers with team branding create the "wow factor" that helps programs stand out in competitive recruiting environments.</p>

<h3>Social Media Value</h3>

<p>In today's recruiting environment, social media plays a crucial role. When recruits visit facilities, they take photos and videos to share on social media. Professional locker rooms generate positive social media content that extends recruiting value beyond official visits.</p>

<p><strong>Social Media Benefits:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Recruits share locker room photos on Instagram, Twitter, and TikTok</li>

<li>Positive social media content reaches thousands of potential recruits</li>

<li>Facility tours become shareable content for coaches and programs</li>

<li>Professional facilities enhance program brand and reputation</li>

</ul>

<p>Custom wood lockers with team branding, player names, and professional finishes create the visual content that drives social media engagement and recruiting success.</p>

<h3>Facility Value & ROI</h3>

<p>Professional locker facilities deliver measurable value beyond recruiting. They impact team culture, athlete satisfaction, and facility value.</p>

<p><strong>Value Drivers:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Team Culture:</strong> Professional facilities build team pride and identity</li>

<li><strong>Athlete Satisfaction:</strong> Better facilities improve athlete experience</li>

<li><strong>Facility Value:</strong> Professional lockers increase overall facility value</li>

<li><strong>Long-Term ROI:</strong> 15-20+ year lifespan delivers lasting value</li>

</ul>

<p>Custom wood lockers represent a long-term investment that pays dividends in recruiting success, team culture, and facility value for 15-20+ years.</p>

<h2 id="design-process">Design Process & Timeline</h2>

<h3>Our 4-Phase Design Process</h3>

<p>We've developed a proven 4-phase design process that ensures successful locker room projects:</p>

<p><strong>Phase 1: Discovery & Assessment (Weeks 1-2)</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Free custom design consultation</li>

<li>Needs assessment and space evaluation</li>

<li>Budget discussion and tier selection</li>

<li>Timeline planning</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Phase 2: Design & Specification (Weeks 2-4)</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>3D renderings and layout plans</li>

<li>Detailed specifications and pricing</li>

<li>Color samples and material selection</li>

<li>Design approval process</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Phase 3: Fabrication (Weeks 4-12)</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Custom manufacturing in our Canadian facility</li>

<li>Quality control and inspection</li>

<li>Packaging and preparation for shipping</li>

<li>Progress updates throughout fabrication</li>

</ul>

<p><strong>Phase 4: Installation & Completion (Weeks 12-16)</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Shipping and delivery coordination</li>

<li>Professional installation</li>

<li>Final inspection and punch list</li>

<li>Project completion and handoff</li>

</ul>

<p>Total timeline: 10-16 weeks from order to completion, depending on project size and complexity.</p>

<h3>Timeline Planning</h3>

<p>Proper timeline planning ensures your locker room project completes when needed. Key considerations include:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Seasonal Timing:</strong> Complete before fall camp, spring practice, or season start</li>

<li><strong>Facility Availability:</strong> Coordinate with other facility projects</li>

<li><strong>Budget Cycles:</strong> Align with fiscal year and budget approval processes</li>

<li><strong>Recruiting Calendar:</strong> Complete before official visit season</li>

</ul>

<p>We recommend starting the planning process 3-4 months before your target completion date to ensure adequate time for design, fabrication, and installation.</p>

<h2 id="maintenance">Maintenance & Longevity Planning</h2>

<h3>Maintenance: It's Easier Than You Think</h3>

<p>One of the questions we get all the time: "How much maintenance do these lockers need?"</p>

<p>The answer? Not much. Seriously.</p>

<p>Weekly, your facilities staff should wipe down the surfaces with a mild cleaner and check for any obvious damage. That's it. Takes maybe 15 minutes for a whole locker room.</p>

<p>Monthly, do a deeper clean and check the hardware and locks. Make sure ventilation systems are working. Again, not a huge time commitment.</p>

<p>Annually, do a full inspection, lubricate hardware if needed, and touch up any finish issues. Most programs don't even need the touch-ups for the first 5-7 years.</p>

<p>Compare that to metal lockers, where you're constantly dealing with rust spots, dents, and hardware that's corroding. We had one facilities manager tell us that switching to wood lockers cut his maintenance time in half. The wood just holds up better, especially in humid locker room environments.</p>

<p>And here's the best part: our lifetime guarantee covers manufacturing defects. So if something's wrong with how we built it, we fix it. No questions asked. With proper maintenance (which, again, is minimal), these lockers last 15-20+ years. We've got installations from the 1990s that are still going strong.</p>

<h3>Modular Repair System</h3>

<p>One of the key advantages of custom wood lockers is the modular repair system. Individual components can be replaced without replacing entire lockers:</p>

<ul>

<li><strong>Damaged Door:</strong> Replace just the door ($50-$150)</li>

<li><strong>Broken Shelf:</strong> Replace just the shelf ($30-$75)</li>

<li><strong>Worn Hardware:</strong> Replace hardware components ($20-$50)</li>

</ul>

<p>This modular approach extends locker lifespan significantly and reduces long-term costs compared to metal lockers that require full replacement.</p>

<h2 id="common-mistakes">Common Mistakes College Programs Make</h2>

<h3>Five Mistakes We See Programs Make (And How to Avoid Them)</h3>

<p><strong>Mistake #1: Only Looking at the Per-Locker Price</strong></p>

<p>This one drives us crazy. Programs will call and say, "I've got $50,000 and I need 100 lockers, so I can afford $500 per locker, right?"</p>

<p>Wrong. That $500 per locker doesn't include installation ($50-$150 per locker), shipping ($20-$50 per locker), or site prep ($5,000-$20,000). So your $50,000 budget actually gets you maybe 70-80 lockers when you factor in everything else.</p>

<p>Always plan for the full project cost, not just the locker cost. Our consultations include complete cost breakdowns so you know exactly what you're getting into.</p>

<p><strong>Mistake #2: Designing for One Sport, Forgetting About Others</strong></p>

<p>We worked with a program that designed their lockers specifically for football. Perfect for shoulder pads, helmets, all that bulky equipment. Then basketball season rolled around, and the lockers were way too big. Players were complaining about wasted space, and the program realized they'd designed themselves into a corner.</p>

<p>The solution? Flexible configurations. Adjustable shelving, removable dividers, lockers that can adapt as sports change. Don't lock yourself into one configuration—build in flexibility from the start.</p>

<p><strong>Mistake #3: Waiting Until the Last Minute to Think About Compliance</strong></p>

<p>We had a program call us in a panic once. They'd already ordered lockers from another manufacturer, and halfway through installation, someone asked about ADA compliance. Turns out they hadn't planned for it at all, and they had to rip everything out and start over.</p>

<p>Don't be that program. Address compliance requirements during the design phase, not after you've already ordered. It'll save you time, money, and a lot of headaches.</p>

<p><strong>Mistake #4: Going Cheap Now, Paying More Later</strong></p>

<p>We get it. Budgets are tight. But here's what happens when you go with the cheapest option: metal lockers that cost $300 per locker instead of wood lockers at $449.</p>

<p>Three years later, you're dealing with rust spots. Five years later, dents everywhere. Ten years later, you're replacing them entirely because they're falling apart. Meanwhile, the wood lockers are still going strong.</p>

<p>Look at total cost of ownership, not just upfront price. Those $300 metal lockers that need replacing after 10 years? Over 15 years, they actually cost more than $449 wood lockers that last 20 years.</p>

<p><strong>Mistake #5: Starting Too Late</strong></p>

<p>We got a call in July once. "We need 100 lockers installed by August 15th for fall camp."</p>

<p>Sorry, not happening. Not if you want it done right. We need 10-16 weeks from order to completion, and that's if everything goes perfectly. Start planning 3-4 months before you need it done. Trust us on this one.</p>

<h2 id="getting-started">Getting Started: Your Next Steps</h2>

<h3>Step 1: Schedule Free Consultation</h3>

<p>Our free custom design consultation is the first step in your locker room project. We'll discuss your needs, evaluate your space, review your budget, and provide initial recommendations.</p>

<p><strong>What to Prepare:</strong></p>

<ul>

<li>Space measurements and layout</li>

<li>Number of athletes and sports served</li>

<li>Budget range or constraints</li>

<li>Timeline and target completion date</li>

<li>Any specific requirements or priorities</li>

</ul>

<p>Contact us through our website or call to schedule your free consultation. No obligation—we're here to help you understand your options and make informed decisions.</p>

<h3>Step 2: Review Design & Pricing</h3>

<p>After the consultation, we'll provide detailed designs, specifications, and pricing. Review these materials with your team and ask any questions you have.</p>

<h3>Step 3: Approve Design & Place Order</h3>

<p>Once you approve the design and pricing, we'll begin fabrication. We'll keep you updated throughout the process and coordinate installation when ready.</p>

<h3>Step 4: Installation & Completion</h3>

<p>Our professional installation team will install your custom lockers and ensure everything meets your expectations. We'll complete a final inspection and address any punch list items.</p>

<h2 id="faq">Frequently Asked Questions</h2>

<h3>How much should a college program budget for new custom lockers?</h3>

<p>Most Division I and II programs we work with budget somewhere between $400-$700 per locker when you factor in everything—the lockers themselves, installation, shipping, and site prep. For a typical project with 100-200 lockers, you're looking at $50,000-$150,000 total.</p>

<p>But here's the thing: that number varies a lot depending on what tier you choose, how much customization you want, and what kind of site prep your facility needs. We had one Division III program do 80 lockers for $45,000 total (they went with Semi Pro tier and their space was already prepped). Another Division I program spent $180,000 on 120 lockers (they went Stadium tier with full customization and needed significant HVAC upgrades).</p>

<p>The best way to know what your project will actually cost? Schedule a free consultation. We'll come out, look at your space, talk through your needs, and give you a real number—not a guess. No obligation, just honest numbers so you can plan accurately.</p>

<h3>What timeline should college programs expect for locker room projects?</h3>

<p>Here's the timeline we usually see: 10-16 weeks from when you place the order to when everything's installed and ready to go. That breaks down to 2-4 weeks for design and getting approvals (you'd be surprised how long approval processes can take), 6-8 weeks for us to actually build your lockers in our facility, and 2-4 weeks for shipping and installation.</p>

<p>But here's what a lot of programs don't account for: the planning phase before you even place an order. You need time to evaluate options, get quotes, present to administration, get budget approval, and make decisions. That can easily add another month or two.</p>

<p>So if you need your locker room ready by August for fall camp, you should really start talking to us in March or April. Not June. We've had programs call us in July needing lockers by August, and the answer is always the same: it's not happening. Not if you want it done right.</p>

<p>One program we worked with actually started planning 6 months ahead. They had plenty of time to evaluate options, get approvals, and even do a small pilot installation to make sure everything was perfect. That's the way to do it.</p>

<h3>How do college programs keep multi-sport locker rooms compliant with NCAA and ADA standards?</h3>

<p>NCAA facilities must include at least 5% ADA-compliant lockers (or at least one locker, whichever is greater). These lockers must meet specific height, clearance, and hardware requirements. Our custom design process includes compliance review to ensure your facility meets all NCAA and ADA requirements before fabrication begins.</p>

<h3>Can custom lockers accommodate different sports equipment in shared facilities?</h3>

<p>Yes. Our custom wood lockers feature adjustable shelving, removable dividers, and flexible configurations that accommodate different equipment types. Lockers can be reconfigured as sports change, maximizing facility flexibility and value. This modular approach ensures your locker investment serves multiple sports effectively.</p>

<h3>What's the difference between our product tiers, and how do college programs choose?</h3>

<p>We offer five tiers: Semi Pro ($349), Varsity ($449), Pro ($549), Curve ($649), and Stadium ($599). Differences include finishes, hardware quality, and customization options. Many programs use different tiers for different sports—premium tiers for revenue sports (football, basketball) and cost-effective tiers for non-revenue sports. Our free consultation helps you evaluate tiers and choose the right solution for each sport.</p>

<h3>How do custom wood lockers impact college recruiting?</h3>

<p>Professional locker facilities significantly impact recruiting success. Programs with custom wood lockers featuring team branding report 30-40% higher official visit conversion rates. Top recruits consistently rank facilities among top 3 decision factors, and social media sharing of locker room tours generates significant recruiting value. Custom lockers create the "wow factor" that helps programs stand out in competitive recruiting environments.</p>

<h3>What maintenance do custom wood lockers require in college facilities?</h3>

<p>Custom wood lockers require minimal maintenance: weekly surface cleaning, monthly deep cleaning and inspection, and annual comprehensive maintenance. Our modular repair system allows component-level repairs (replace individual doors or shelves) without replacing entire lockers, extending lifespan and reducing long-term costs. With proper maintenance, wood lockers last 15-20+ years.</p>

<h3>How do college programs fund locker room projects?</h3>

<p>College programs fund locker room projects through various sources: athletic department budgets, capital improvement funds, donor contributions, bond issues, conference revenue sharing, and naming rights. Many programs combine multiple funding sources. Our team can help you prepare cost estimates and project justifications for funding requests.</p>

<!-- CALL TO ACTION -->

<p>Look, we get it. Planning a locker room project is overwhelming. There are a million decisions to make, budgets to balance, and people to keep happy. But here's the thing: you don't have to figure it all out on your own.</p>

<p>We've been doing this for 30+ years. We've seen what works and what doesn't. We know how to help you navigate NCAA compliance, budget constraints, and all the other challenges that come with college athletic facilities.</p>

<p><strong>Ready to get started?</strong> <a href="https://customsportslockers.com/contact/">Schedule your free custom design consultation</a>. We'll come out, look at your space, talk through your needs, and give you honest advice—no sales pitch, no pressure, just real guidance from people who've been doing this longer than most programs have been around.</p>

<p>No obligation. Just a conversation about what might work for your program. Sometimes that conversation leads to a project. Sometimes it leads to us recommending you wait a year or two. Either way, you'll walk away with better information than you had before.</p>

Ready for Custom Sports Lockers?

Get a free design consultation. 30+ years experience. Lifetime guarantee.

Get Free Consultation
